news.title: Infosys invests $560M in two US tech firms, marking its highest M&A spend in a year.

news.title:
Infosys to acquire Stratus, enhancing its insurance sector position with 450+ domain experts.
The deal integrates Stratus with Infosys Topaz AI, Cobalt cloud, and Guidewire Software.
news.summary:
Infosys to acquire Stratus, enhancing its insurance sector position with 450+ domain experts.
The deal integrates Stratus with Infosys Topaz AI, Cobalt cloud, and Guidewire Software.

news.title:
Infosys announces acquisitions of Optimum Healthcare IT for up to $465M and Stratus Global LLC for up to $95M.
These acquisitions aim to bolster Infosys' capabilities in healthcare digital transformation and AI-driven insurance solutions.
news.summary:
Infosys announces acquisitions of Optimum Healthcare IT for up to $465M and Stratus Global LLC for up to $95M.
These acquisitions aim to bolster Infosys' capabilities in healthcare digital transformation and AI-driven insurance solutions.

news.title:
BoFA sees rupee weakness boosting Q4 IT earnings; Infosys EBIT margin guidance at 20-22%.
HCLTech's FY27 revenue growth forecast at 2.5-5.5%; forex gains to aid profitability.
news.summary:
BoFA sees rupee weakness boosting Q4 IT earnings; Infosys EBIT margin guidance at 20-22%.
HCLTech's FY27 revenue growth forecast at 2.5-5.5%; forex gains to aid profitability.

news.title:
Infosys partners with Producers Savings Bank, Philippines to enhance digital lending.
The upgrade uses Finacle Core Banking and Origination software from Infosys.
news.summary:

news.title:
JPMorgan's Q4FY26 Indian IT preview highlights revenue guidance importance; Infosys FY27 growth forecast at 2-5%.
Q4 results: Infosys down 0.7%, TCS up 0.6%, HCL down 1.6%; INR depreciation offsets earnings downgrades.
news.summary:
JPMorgan's Q4FY26 Indian IT preview highlights revenue guidance importance; Infosys FY27 growth forecast at 2-5%.
Q4 results: Infosys down 0.7%, TCS up 0.6%, HCL down 1.6%; INR depreciation offsets earnings downgrades.

news.title:
UBS sees Infosys, HCLTech, and Persistent as top IT picks amid sector de-rating, citing better risk-reward profiles.
UBS downgraded Mphasis, upgraded Tech Mahindra, and initiated Neutral on Coforge while highlighting AI-driven challenges in IT.
news.summary:
UBS sees Infosys, HCLTech, and Persistent as top IT picks amid sector de-rating, citing better risk-reward profiles.
UBS downgraded Mphasis, upgraded Tech Mahindra, and initiated Neutral on Coforge while highlighting AI-driven challenges in IT.

news.title:
HDFC Bank led a ₹1 tn market cap erosion among top 5 firms; Sensex dipped 0.04%.
Reliance Industries gained ₹45.9k cr, while Bharti Airtel, SBI, LIC, Infosys also rose.
news.summary:
HDFC Bank led a ₹1 tn market cap erosion among top 5 firms; Sensex dipped 0.04%.
Reliance Industries gained ₹45.9k cr, while Bharti Airtel, SBI, LIC, Infosys also rose.

news.title:
Post-Accenture Q2, analysts see AI as growth enabler but not a demand revival driver for Indian IT.
Infosys, Mphasis, Coforge, and Wipro gain; selective stock picks advised amid geopolitical and macro concerns.
news.summary:
Post-Accenture Q2, analysts see AI as growth enabler but not a demand revival driver for Indian IT.

This move strengthens its Asia Pacific presence and expands its client base.

news.title:
Accenture's Q2 results show limited support for IT sector; Indian IT stocks, including Infosys, TCS, fell 1.5%-4%.
Brokerages remain cautious; Nomura prefers Infosys, Coforge, eClerx; TCS down 10% in March; AI seen as tailwind.
news.summary:
Accenture's Q2 results show limited support for IT sector; Indian IT stocks, including Infosys, TCS, fell 1.5%-4%.
Brokerages remain cautious; Nomura prefers Infosys, Coforge, eClerx; TCS down 10% in March; AI seen as tailwind.

Fed cites persistent inflation, Middle East tensions, and US tariff policies as concerns.

The new entity will be wholly owned by Infosys; further details will follow as per regulations.

news.title:

India VIX drops over 4%. Bharti Airtel and L&T also drive Nifty gains.

news.title:
HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, and Infosys were top mutual fund buys in February, while SBI and Bajaj Finance saw selling.
Mutual funds remain overweight on pharma, e-commerce, and capital goods, while underweight on private banks, oil & gas, and IT services.
news.summary:
HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, and Infosys were top mutual fund buys in February, while SBI and Bajaj Finance saw selling.
Mutual funds remain overweight on pharma, e-commerce, and capital goods, while underweight on private banks, oil & gas, and IT services.

news.title:
Nifty IT rose over 4%, led by large-cap IT stocks like Infosys, Tech Mahindra, HCLTech, Wipro, and TCS.
These IT stocks gained between 3% and 5%, driving market optimism.

news.title:
Sensex surged 500 pts, Nifty 50 crossed 23,700 on easing oil prices.
Markets gained Rs 5 lakh cr in value as investor sentiment improved.
